title: Use vetKey to share transport key context between Dashboard and Compose

requirements.text: Introduce a frontend "vetKey" mechanism that allows the app to persist and retrieve the caller's transport keypair across navigation so that a transport key generated/rotated on the Dashboard is available on the Compose screen without requiring regeneration in the new page/tab.

quotes: use vet Key to pass between dashborad and compose

acceptanceCriteria: After generating or rotating a transport key on the Dashboard, navigating to the Compose page in the same session uses the existing local transport keypair (no "key not available on this device/tab" error caused by page-level state reset).
The transport private key is never sent to the backend; only the public key is registered as it is today.
The persisted transport keypair can be restored after route changes and full page reloads within the same browser session (sessionStorage-based persistence is acceptable).

requirements.text: Update the Dashboard UI to surface the current vetKey and provide an explicit action to navigate to Compose while passing the vetKey value as a URL parameter (or hash parameter) so Compose can load the correct persisted transport key context.

quotes: use vet Key to pass between dashborad and compose

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard provides a way to copy the vetKey to clipboard and/or a button that navigates to the Compose route while including the vetKey in the URL.
If the user opens the generated Compose link in a new tab in the same browser session, Compose can still resolve the vetKey and load the transport keypair from session storage.
If no vetKey is available, the Dashboard action is disabled or shows a clear error toast in English.

requirements.text: Update ComposeMessagePage to read the vetKey from the URL (and/or persisted session) and initialize transport key access using that vetKey so that message sending eligibility checks reflect the actual locally-available transport keypair.

quotes: use vet Key to pass between dashborad and compose

acceptanceCriteria: When Compose loads with a valid vetKey in the URL, the page uses it to access the transport keypair and the send eligibility logic ("canSend") can become true without requiring a fresh key generation in Compose.

When Compose loads without a vetKey (or with an invalid vetKey), Compose behaves safely: it does not crash, and it shows the existing diagnostics/error messaging in English indicating the key is unavailable.
The vetKey parameter is handled using the existing URL/session parameter utilities where appropriate (e.g., persisted parameter behavior or hash-secret behavior).

constraints: Do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui (read-only components must be composed, not modified).
Do not modify the immutable auth/actor hooks (frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts/tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx).
Backend must remain a single Motoko actor; no additional canisters or external storage/databases.
Do not transmit or persist the transport private key to the backend; only persist it locally in the browser.
nonGoals: Changing the backend message schema or introducing new backend endpoints for vetKey.
Adding third-party authentication or external key management services.
Implementing real-time synchronization across different devices/browsers for private keys.
